title: Fabrication of Human Skin Equivalents Using Decellularized Extracellular Matrix
author: Sarmin, Connelly
materials: Pig skin (Local butcher, pork belly skin, 50‐100 g)

steps.title: PREPARATION OF DECELLULARIZED EXTRACELLULAR MATRIX FROM PORCINE SKIN
steps.instruction: Protocol 1 describes the method for preparing dECM from porcine skin. The procedure begins with 50‐100 g of skin from pig belly, which can be obtained from a local butcher or abattoir, and it requires 7‐10 days to complete. An overall summary of the workflow is shown in Figure 1, and it is recommended that the entire protocol is reviewed carefully before beginning. Decellularization involves removal of the epidermis with the enzyme Dispase, followed by combined Trypsin and detergent treatment to remove the cells of the dermis. The decellularized dermal tissue is then freeze‐dried and solubilized with Pepsin to yield a 20 mg/ml solution of dECM. The dECM can be portioned into small aliquots and stored at −20°C for up to 1 year. Note: an initial starting mass of 50‐100 g (20 × 10 cm) of porcine skin can be expected to produce approximately 500 ml of soluble dECM.
